fundamentals.name: Sandisk Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Sandisk Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells data storage devices and solutions using NAND flash technology in the United States, Europe, the Middle East, Africa, Asia, and internationally. The company offers solid state drives for desktop and notebook PCs, gaming consoles, and set top boxes; and flash-based embedded storage products for mobile phones, tablets, notebook PCs and other portable and wearable devices, automotive applications, Internet of Things, industrial, and connected home applications, as well as removable cards, universal serial bus drives, and wafers and components. It sells its products to computer manufacturers and original equipment manufacturers, datacenters, private cloud customers, cloud service providers, resellers, distributors, and retailers through its sales personnel, dealers, distributors, retailers, and subsidiaries. Sandisk Corporation was incorporated in 2024 and is based in Milpitas, California.

judgment.summary: Extremely high growth company with strong margins and a low forward P/E, though with significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter (ending 2026-03-31) EPS beat estimate by 59.7%; next earnings date is 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue growth 251.0% YoY — explosive top-line expansion.
Profit margin 34.2% and Operating margin 70.0% — industry-leading profitability.
Forward P/E 8.43x — exceptionally low relative to growth and trailing P/E.
Analyst growth estimates are extremely high for current Q (11375.4%) and FY (2074.4%).
Free cash flow $2.26B TTM — strong cash generation.
Consensus rating is 'buy' with a mean target price slightly above current levels.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 1.50 indicates significant leverage.
Trailing P/E of 50.47 suggests high expectations are already priced in historically.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 16.61 is high, indicating a premium valuation on revenue.
Beta is not provided, making it difficult to assess systematic risk.

news.paragraph: SanDisk (SNDK) is seeing significant positive attention, with its stock up 550% year-to-date and trading around $1,540, prompting discussions about whether to buy, sell, or hold. The company is positioned to benefit from the AI boom, with analysts projecting substantial growth in the current quarter and fiscal year. Recent news highlights a general increase in Wall Street bullishness despite underlying risks, and SNDK is specifically mentioned as a top performer riding the AI wave.

headlines.title: Wall Street Is Getting More Bullish on Stocks Despite Risks—Here's Why
Riding the AI Wave Without Getting Wiped Out
This Alphabet‑Heavy Fund Has a Front‑Row Seat to the AI Boom — and the Market Can See Why It’s Suddenly a Top Performer
Up 550% YTD, SanDisk Stock at $1,540: Buy, Sell or Hold?
Zacks Investment Ideas feature highlights: NVIDIA, QQQ, Micron, SanDisk, Cipher Mining and USO
